description: Fix an unbounded ESM module cache leak in watch mode and a String.replace special-pattern bug in icon generation in the plugin-tools package.

userStories.title: Fix pluginCacheKey to alternate instead of monotonically increment
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want watch mode to alternate the ESM module cache key between 0 and 1 (as the comment claims) so that long dev sessions do not accumulate unbounded module cache entries.
acceptanceCriteria: pluginCacheKey alternates between two values (e.g., pluginCacheKey = 1 - pluginCacheKey, or pluginCacheKey ^= 1) instead of monotonically incrementing
The ESM module cache holds at most 2 entries for the plugin entry point at any time
The comment on lines 44-46 matches the actual behavior
userStories.notes: In platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts:44-47, the comment says 'Alternates between 0 and 1 so the ESM module cache holds at most 2 entries' but the code does pluginCacheKey++ (line 1035), which monotonically increments. Each rebuild creates a new import URL (?t=N) that Node.js caches permanently. Fix: change line 1035 from pluginCacheKey++ to pluginCacheKey = 1 - pluginCacheKey (or pluginCacheKey ^= 1). This makes the cache key alternate between 0 and 1, matching the comment and bounding cache entries to 2.

userStories.title: Fix generateInactiveIcon String.replace $ pattern interpretation
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want inactive icon generation to correctly handle SVG style values containing dollar-sign characters so that the SVG output is not corrupted by String.replace special replacement patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: The inline style replacement in generateInactiveIcon uses a replacement function (second arg to .replace) instead of a replacement string, avoiding $ pattern interpretation
SVG style values containing $&, $1, $', $` are handled correctly without corruption
Existing icon validation tests continue to pass
userStories.notes: In platform/plugin-tools/src/validate-icon.ts:529, the code does `return fullMatch.replace(styleValue, newStyle)`. String.prototype.replace with a string replacement interprets $ sequences specially ($&, $1, $', $`). If newStyle contains dollar signs (possible with certain CSS values), the output will be corrupted. Fix: use a function replacement instead: `return fullMatch.replace(styleValue, () => newStyle)`. The function form does not interpret $ patterns. Tests are in platform/plugin-tools/src/validate-icon.test.ts.
